inspection_evidence: HEAD af070b819dbeb13f38176e45436b7f91bf819f29; tracked worktree was clean before this ignored review artifact was created

src/cli.js:2093-2109 now rejects --repair-linked-copy before guarded and legacy dispatch; test/guarded-run-session.test.js:517-558 covers run, legacy/guarded status, resume, cancel, start, next, reconcile, and merge in human/JSON modes and proves no executions artifact is created
test/guarded-run-session.test.js:983-996 verifies help advertises repair only on the watch-specific usage line; the broad execute usage omits the option

judgment_delta: Initial concern: the accepted repair-flag-silent-outside-watch finding might still exist on a legacy or guarded branch. Final conclusion: resolved, because every documented non-watch execute subcommand now returns typed exit 2 before dispatch and the added test would fail against the pre-fix control flow.

Initial canonicalization concern: preferring sourceRoot might change migration/fallback fingerprints inconsistently. Final conclusion: no regression found, because all normalizeManagedBinding callers supply resolved canonical identities and the new alias fixture covers persistence, status/watch, resume, and cancel.

Initial pass candidate after 36/36 focused and 1126/1126 recorded full-suite success -> needs_changes after path-surface execution showed that the broad repair guard intercepts an unknown subcommand and changes its established exit/error surface; the new table test does not cover this fallback route.

findings.detail: src/cli.js:2098 checks --repair-linked-copy before validating that subcommand is one of the documented execute commands. Consequently `vibepro execute nonsense . --repair-linked-copy --json` exits 2 with repair_linked_copy_not_supported, while the established unknown-command fallback at src/cli.js:2213 exits 1 and renders `Unknown execute command: nonsense`. This changes an adjacent legacy CLI error/exit contract and misclassifies an invalid command as a valid non-watch command with an unsupported option. Restrict the repair guard to the known non-watch subcommand set (or validate the subcommand first), and add a pre-fix-sensitive regression assertion that unknown execute commands still reach the unknown-command path when the flag is present.

resolved_findings.detail: Resolved at af070b8: all documented non-watch execute subcommands reject --repair-linked-copy with typed exit 2 before dispatch, identity resolution, reads, or mutation; help now scopes the option to watch.
